items.headline: Pistons learned a lesson in tough Game 7 loss to Cavaliers
items.blurb: Pistons learned a lesson in tough Game 7 loss to Cavaliers — The Detroit Pistons had high hopes heading into Game 7 of their Eastern Conference semifinal series against the Cleveland Cavaliers, but things went the other way as the team fell hard, losing 125-94.

items.headline: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander's historic second MVP season bests Michael Jordan
items.blurb: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander's historic second MVP season bests Michael Jordan — Further enhancing his already Hall of Fame-caliber career, Shai Gilgeous-Alexander was named the NBA's MVP on Sunday, earning the honor in back-to-back seasons.

items.headline: Cavaliers owner Dan Gilbert creates a home-court advantage on the road
items.blurb: Cavaliers owner Dan Gilbert creates a home-court advantage on the road — The Cleveland Cavaliers won two road games in Detroit to advance to the Eastern Conference Finals. Their owner made sure the Cavs got to see some friendly faces in the Motor City.

items.blurb: Terry Stotts Would Like To Return As Trail Blazers Coach — The Portland Trail Blazers are continuing their search for their next head coach by looking at current NBA assistant coaches. items.headline: Spurs To Play NBA European Games Next Season
items.blurb: Spurs To Play NBA European Games Next Season — San Antonio Spurs superstar Victor Wembanyama is expected to have a homecoming next season. The Spurs are set to play in a pair of regular season games in Europe. The NBA is hosting games in Paris, France and Manchester, England next season. items.headline: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander continues Hall of Fame path with second straight MVP award
items.blurb: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander continues Hall of Fame path with second straight MVP award — The Oklahoma City Thunder are halfway to an NBA championship repeat. Their best player, Shai Gilgeous-Alexander, has already earned his own individual repeat by winning the league's MVP award for the second year in a row.

items.blurb: Jalen Williams Available For Game 1 Of Western Conference Finals — The Oklahoma City Thunder have listed forward Jalen Williams as available to play in Game 1 of the Western Conference Finals. The Thunder open the West Finals on Monday, when they host the San Antonio Spurs. items.headline: Nuggets Expected To Cut Costs This Offseason
items.blurb: Nuggets Expected To Cut Costs This Offseason — The Denver Nuggets have no intention of blowing up their roster and starting over. However, the Nuggets are expected to trim some salary heading into next season. Jonas Valanciunas isn't expected to be back in Denver next season. Though a formal agreement cannot be completed until the offseason, an understanding is in place between both sides to work out a new deal. items.headline: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander Wins 2nd Straight NBA Most Valuable Player Award
items.blurb: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander Wins 2nd Straight NBA Most Valuable Player Award — Shai Gilgeous-Alexander has won his second straight NBA Most Valuable Player award . Gilgeous-Alexander officially received the award on Sunday. Gilgeous-Alexander received 83 out of 100 first place votes, 13 second place votes, three third place votes and one fourth place vote. items.headline: Wolves Pursuing Tim Connelly Contract Extension
items.blurb: Wolves Pursuing Tim Connelly Contract Extension — The Minnesota Timberwolves are moving to retain president of basketball operations Tim Connelly on a contract extension, team sources told The Athletic. Connelly enters the final year of his current deal following Minnesota's second-round exit against the San Antonio Spurs. Despite being considered a candidate for the top basketball operations role

items.headline: Anthony Edwards Defers To Wolves' Management On Closing Gap With Spurs, Thunder
items.blurb: Anthony Edwards Defers To Wolves' Management On Closing Gap With Spurs, Thunder — Anthony Edwards had no clear answer Friday when asked how the Minnesota Timberwolves can close the distance between themselves and the Western Conference's two elite teams, the Oklahoma City Thunder and San Antonio Spurs. The question, posed after a 139-109 Game 6 loss to San Antonio, landed without a clean response.

items.headline: Evan Mobley: Unstoppable in blowout victory
items.blurb: Evan Mobley: Unstoppable in blowout victory — Mobley recorded 21 points (7-10 FG, 1-4 3Pt, 6-11 FT), 12 rebounds, six assists, two blocks and two steals in 36 minutes during Sunday's 125-94 win over the Pistons in Game 7 of the Eastern Conference Semifinals. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: Jarrett Allen: Dominates in Game 7 win
items.blurb: Jarrett Allen: Dominates in Game 7 win — Allen ended with 23 points (8-14 FG, 7-13 FT), seven rebounds, one assist, one block and one steal across 25 minutes during Sunday's 125-94 win over the Pistons in Game 7 of the Eastern Conference Semifinals. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.blurb: Naz Reid Says Wolves Need To Be 'Less Moody, More Selfless' — In the wake of the Minnesota Timberwolves loss to the San Antonio Spurs in the second round of the NBA Playoffs, Naz Reid was asked what his team needs to do to catch the Spurs and the Oklahoma City Thunder. items.blurb: Warriors Want Draymond Green To Return But Say Decision Is Up To Him — Veteran forward Draymond Green holds a player option for next season. items.blurb: Donovan Mitchell: Scores 26 in Game 7 — Mitchell posted 26 points (10-22 FG, 2-6 3Pt, 4-6 FT), eight assists, six rebounds, one steal and a block in 31 minutes during Sunday's Game 7 win over the Pistons in the Eastern Conference Semifinals. items.blurb: De'Aaron Fox: Iffy for Game 1 — Fox (ankle) is questionable for Monday's Game 1 of the Western Conference Finals against the Thunder, Paul Garcia of ProjectSpurs.com reports. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.blurb: Knicks' OG Anunoby makes revealing comments about injury ahead of ECF — Despite missing the last two games of the Eastern Conference semifinals, New York Knicks forward OG Anunoby isn't all that worried about his recent hamstring injury.

items.blurb: Mike Conley Plans To Keep Playing; Bones Hyland Would Like To Stay With Wolves — Veteran point guard Mike Conley is shrugging off retirement talk for at least one more year. items.blurb: Elliot Cadeau Withdrawing From Draft, Returning To Michigan — Guard Elliot Cadeau is withdrawing from the 2026 NBA Draft and returning to Michigan. for his senior season. items.blurb: Jrue Holiday Hopes To Remain With Trail Blazers — Veteran guard Jrue Holiday has moved around a lot in recent years. In the last few years, Holiday has been traded from the Milwaukee Bucks to the Portland Trail Blazers to the Boston Celtics then back to the Trail Blazers. items.blurb: Kings May Pursue Mitchell Robinson In Free Agency — The New York Knicks hope to re-sign Mitchell Robinson, who is entering unrestricted free agency.
